dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 77, responding for a possible stroke.
Hi, I'm Josh, a paramedic from the [CITY] Rescue. What made you call 911 today?
He suddenly started having trouble on the left side of his body. We're his caretakers at a nursing facility.
What kind of trouble are you noticing on the left side?
He can lift his left arm, but it's drifting down. He also can't move his right side at all.
Mr. Thomas, can you hear us? What is your name?
Thomas...not sure about the time yet.
Mr. Thomas, are your pupils reactive?
PERRL confirmed bilaterally.
Mr. Thomas, slowly raise your right arm and then the left arm for me.
He can only lift the left one without drifting; the right arm fails to rise.
Airway is clear and maintainable, no signs of obstruction.
Can I check your chest for breathing effort?
Please proceed.
Equal chest rise and fall, clear bilateral breath sounds detected.
Let me check your pulse and skin condition.
Peripheral pulses present and regular. Skin is warm, pink, and dry.
Mr. Thomas, can you describe the exact symptom you're experiencing on the left side?
He started having left-sided weakness a week ago after his stroke. Today's left drifting is worse, and he can't move his right side at all.
Have you had any prior strokes or other medical issues?
Yes, he had a stroke a week ago and is diabetic. He also has hypertension, Hepatitis C, and a history of major depression.
Can you share his age or any other personal details maybe?
He's a 62-year-old.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how severe is your left-sided weakness right now?
It's a mild 3.
Mr. Thomas, we'll now assess your skin for any changes in color or texture.
Skin is dry, pink, and normal in color, no rashes or scars noted.
May we shine a light into your eyes to check your pupils?
I suppose.
PERRL confirmed bilaterally, no lymphadenopathy or neck masses noted.
Your nose and mouth appear unremarkable. Any drainage or obstruction observed here?
Nares clear, oral cavity free of secretions. No bite marks noted inside the mouth or throat.
No signs of swelling or deformity in ears and throat. Normal gross apperance of hearing or nasal passages noted.
Mr. Thomas, are there any lumps or tenderness in your neck besides this condition itself?
No noticeable jugular venous distention. Trachea is midline with no deviation observed upon inspection.
Do you have any severe pain in your limbs from movement restriction, or is it a gradual sensation without sharp pain involved?
He doesn't show signs of pain, just confusion and immobility on the right with left arm drifting.
Pulse is 60, strong and regular with bounding peripheral pulses noted upon palpation of radial artery.
Respiratory rate is 20, without signs of distress or increased work of breathing observed here during this check.

Blood pressure is 181 over 84, slightly elevated but overall stable at time of initial evaluation by myself as backup if check in charge joins later steps for confirmation if needed for critical conditions such as suspected stroke protocol because he has AMS here now and left side drift including impaired verbal responses perhaps due to before stroke incidents happening now with no chance for self-awareness when responding.

EKG is in sinus rhythm, no signs of acute ST-segment changes or arrhythmias.
Let me place an ECG lead for heart rhythm assessment.

12-lead ECG obtained, results normal for a 40+ male with sinus rhythm showing no ST-segment elevation or infarction findings consistent with baseline heart profile already known from patient history and protocol orders to check at least once before transport per established practices in this system.
Would you allow us to attempt an IV on one of your arms or legs as part regular management potentially required for stroke interventions?
